feed.title: AO3 - Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne (Filtered)
feed.subtitle: Latest works for Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne on Archive of Our Own [English, No ABO]

entry.title: The House That Keeps Things
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 38,619Chapters: 9/9Kudos: 0

Summary: Wayne Manor is haunted. Everyone in Gotham knows this. What no one knows — not even the world's greatest detective — is that the haunting is not metaphorical. Thomas and Martha Wayne died inside their own home, and under the rules that govern the house, anyone who dies on Wayne grounds stays. Bruce Wayne has spent his entire adult life in a house full of ghosts. He doesn't know because he can't afford to know. Every anomaly has a rational explanation: the silverware moved because Alfred reorganized. The cold spots are antiquated HVAC. The feeling of being watched is just trauma. Alfred has known since the beginning. He is a man who has been quietly setting three places at the table for forty years. And then Clark Kent walks through the front door, and his senses — the ones that can hear a heartbeat from orbit — register something. A SuperBat/American Horror Story (Murder House) crossover. Story 1 of 5.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: Player One: The New Bat In Town
entry.summary:
Author: Anonymous

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 20Chapters: 1/20Kudos: 1

Summary: Bruce has been in Gotham for a little over a year after being thrown into this universe by one great Galacta and Booster Gold. Having built his own little home in the depths of the Narrows, he is happy to give a helping hand to the people of Gotham, even if it is the vigilante variety.-----Bruce and his friend Tony get isekied into the game worlds their friends made before dying. Except no one told them they weren't game worlds and instead alternate universes. Once they were sent in to live their lives after having arrived and being stabilized. and maybe find love?orSomehow, the batfam has found an adorable Bruce clone helping out the Narrows and have adopted him.

entry.title: The Raven Roosts in the Cornflower Blue
entry.summary:
Author: Aurora2Sunset

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 35,830Chapters: 7/?Kudos: 202

Summary: Someone new moved to Smallville, in the house on the other side of the Kent farm. Clark meets the new kid, Bruce Wayne, and his first impression isn't great. He thinks that Bruce is spoiled and rude and out of place. Maybe that's why he blows up at him. But he still saves him when he's in trouble, that's just the kind of person that Clark is. After a while they develop a tentative kind of friendship over the summer. Bruce isn't like anyone else that Clark knows, and he feels connected to him in all of his out of place-ness. His own feelings for the other take him by surprise, he only hopes that he can stomp them down before he ruins their friendship over them. - Smallville AU where Bruce spends the summers there and befriends Clark long before either of them become superheroes.

entry.title: I don't love you.
entry.summary:
Author: ClarkKents_Biceps

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 9,496Chapters: 4/?Kudos: 23

Summary: “I said bullshit Bruce. You’re telling me those nights I had dinner in the manor, the time you let me wear you’re fucking clothes, the laughs we had? You’re telling me there was nothing there? You’re telling me that you didn’t feel anything?” “I didn’t. And you know what? I don't think I ever will.” Bruce gritted out. “Fuck.” Clark whispered softly as he sobbed, “how am I supposed to un-love you Bruce? Tell me? Spare me.” ~Basically, a story where Clark LOVES Bruce but ends up getting rejected. Years late,r Bruce realises he loves Clark only to find out it's too late and he got away.~LOTS OF ANGST!

entry.title: Don't Dream it's Over
entry.summary:
Author: NightTwink73

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 23,814Chapters: 8/20Kudos: 42

Summary: Eight months after Clark and Lois’s divorce, a breakup letter appears on Commissioner Gordon’s desk—addressed to Batman. But everything about it is wrong. Dear Batman,I can’t do this anymore. Whatever this is between us—it isn’t fair to either of us. You’ve always kept me at a distance, and I told myself I was okay with that, but I’m not.I need something real. Something you were never willing to give me.Don’t try to find me. I’m safe, and I won’t be coming back.I love you. Goodbye.— Superman As Bruce investigates, it quickly becomes clear: maybe the letter isn’t lying about Clark not coming back—and that’s a terrifying thought. Meanwhile, Clark wakes up in a dream life, living beside the man he’s never dared to love… trapped in a prison disguised as perfection.

entry.title: Aphelion
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 30,442Chapters: 13/13Kudos: 55

Summary: The thing about Lex Luthor's parties is that they are an exercise in applied pressure. Bruce Wayne arrives at a Metropolis gala with a device in his pocket and an exit strategy in his head. He leaves with neither, and a persistent frequency he cannot locate or turn off. Clark Kent is warm, direct, and completely unimpressed by power. He takes Amtrak. He tips well. He eats fermented shark with the serenity of a man at peace with the universe. Bruce builds a file. The threat assessment field remains blank. This is a problem. — A BvS divergence. Close third Bruce POV. Physics metaphors and slow burn throughout. Explicit in the back half.

entry.title: But you can't take the circus from the boy
entry.summary:
Author: Gotham_Blight

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,943Chapters: 1/?Kudos: 52

Summary: Damian Wayne has everything he's ever wanted. A beautiful husband. A precious child. A title of his own. Even his siblings, having experienced horrors beyond their years, are growing as people alongside him. But with everything in his life moving so smoothly, he can't help but wonder when the other shoe will drop. Happiness isn't something that comes easily to Demons, after all.

entry.title: You can take the boy from the circus
entry.summary:
Author: Gotham_Blight

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 97,410Chapters: 21/21Kudos: 665

Summary: Damian Al-Ghul Wayne had everything at one point. He was a prince. Ibn al Xu'ffasch. Son of the bat. Heir to the demon. He had never asked to be ripped away from everything that he knew, from the warmth of his home, his culture, his palace in Nanda Parbat. He had never asked to be shipped to a gloomy, grey city. Even when his father kept taking in orphans and ruining his plans for inheritance, Damian stood steadfast. But then his father died, leaving behind his newest orphan. A tiny acrobat with watery blue eyes and black curls, who spoke no English. Damian knew the boy never asked to be ripped away from everything that he knew, from the warmth of his home, his culture, his circus troupe. Damian knew he needed to be there for him.

entry.title: The Buttered Cat Paradox
entry.summary:
Author: writersagainstwritersblock

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 118,055Chapters: 38/?Kudos: 1,499

Summary: “No comment.”“You don’t know what I was going to ask,” Clark said with a hint of a smile. “Maybe I just wanted to congratulate you on catching the Film Freak.”Bruce gave him a flat look.“Or maybe I think I can help.”“With a case of vandalism?”Clark stepped in front of him. “It’s not vandalism and you know it, detective.”“And what makes you say that?”“If it were vandalism it would be something a lot more juvenile. To the point. It wouldn’t claim there were seventeen missing kids. That’s public outcry.”“Is that so?”“That and I tried to do a story on the growing number of homeless children last month and I got shut down. According to the last count, the numbers have been decreasing, but there’s been no change in policies, no extra funding to CPS, or shelters, or even an increase in the number of inmates in the juvenile detention center. So where are they going?” Detective Bruce Wayne may have the highest solve rate in Gotham, but if he wants to solve this case, he’ll need the help of an elusive street kid, and while Dick Grayson always lands on his feet, he usually lands himself right into trouble.

entry.title: North Star
entry.summary:
Author: psychobabblers

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 20,739Chapters: 10/?Kudos: 1,210

Summary: When Superman returns to Metropolis from a space trip, he quickly gets up to speed on the past few years, especially the recent disaster in Gotham and the rise of a mysterious figure whom people are calling Batman. He wants to help, but Gotham is hurting right now, and Superman may do more harm than good. In the end, it may be Clark Kent who is needed to save the day.

entry.title: Sabbatical
entry.summary:
Author: xoxo_yours

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,366Chapters: 1/?Kudos: 12

Summary: After a dangerous leak puts their identities at risk, the Justice League and their allies are forced into a months-long lockdown in a single, secure, and extremely luxurious safehouse. No missions. No patrols. No leaving. Cut off from their normal lives and forced to drop their aliases, they’re left with nothing but each other. What could possibly go wrong? Alternatively: everyone is stuck in a house together and it's going terribly.

entry.summary:

entry.title: A Little Spider Caught up in a Hood
entry.summary:
Author: FayeVilla418

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 7,221Chapters: 4/?Kudos: 523

Summary: Peter wakes up to green. He died. But he's alive. And in another universe too, neat. He breaks out of the glass tube He was in, goes feral, attacking the scientists that had him locked up. He has turned into more of a spider, he never used to have fangs or venom. The door gets broken down while Peter's jaws are still clamped down on a quickly fading flailing kidnapper. It's Red Hood. Red Hood takes in the situation and decides to take Peter, keep him at a safe house, under lock and key, away from the bats, who had gotten to the Lazura's pit just after Jason had left. Peter tries to accept these new changes and this new universe, knowing that there was almost absolutely no way back. And no one would look for him either. At least he has Jason. But the bats break into the safe house Pete's staying at and immedietly assume that Peter is Jason's bf. Anytime they try to deny it, it just feeds into their assumption. Oh well.

entry.title: the best parts of lonely
entry.summary:
Author: Ferox

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 6,876Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 178

Summary: Clark Kent has a pretty great thing going for him. He's the backup goalie for the Gotham Blades, the worst team in NHL history, and he's working on his journalism degree. He never has to worry about money and he's got plenty of free time to be Superman. It's all going really well. Until Bruce Wayne ruins everything. “When I asked you what you wanted for your birthday, you said you wanted the Blades to,” Bruce used air quotes which shouldn't be endearing, “win an ‘effing’ game for once in your life.” "What,” Jason barks out a laugh, “you’re gonna bribe a ref or somethin’?” “Goodness no!” Bruce cries out dramatically clutching his hands to his chest, utterly scandalized. “What do you take me for? A criminal? Of course not, Jason. I would never! I am a good influence.” Jason continues to glare at him, clearly waiting for something. “I bought the team,” Bruce announces.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: Dark Days and Lost Suns
entry.summary:
Author: Athena_Faymont

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 251,402Chapters: 30/30Kudos: 328

Summary: Clark admires Batman, the man who has given up civilian life to protect everyone else's, the tough, sharp, and compassionate man. Bruce admires Superman, the alien who, despite being able to dominate them all by force, has decided to take care of a planet that isn't his own.And, even with these two flawed principles, neither of them can imagine how alone the other feels... and how alone they themselves are.This is a long story, the superbat fanfic I've always wanted to write about how, within the canon, one day something changes and they find each other.If you want to join me on this adventure, we'll be here every Friday![Puedes encontrarlo en español en mi perfil.]Work completed.

entry.title: Forever and evermore (For Eternity)
entry.summary:
Author: starfishingbythehill

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,725Chapters: 4/7Kudos: 5

Summary: “Come on Clark, it isn’t even that scary!” Clark’s face reminds him of the statue in his mother’s garden, the one over the fountain. The scrunched nose, the distant eyes, the thin lips. Displeasure and acceptance both. He really doesn’t understand what’s wrong. “We climb higher in the barn.” “It isn’t the same thing !” Clark denies. “I can’t do it Bruce, I’m not allowed to. It would make Ma sad.” “You’re such a wuss.” Bruce rolls his eyes and jumps down the tree. Clark pouts, crossing his arms in front of himself. Bruce rolls his eyes harder. Clark never breaks the rules and he never seems happy about it. Anyway, he won’t even stay mad for long, they’re best friends so it’s okay. “Let’s go see the horses. Tommy is teaching me how to ride.” Clark smiles at him then. See? What did he say, it’s okay. They can’t stay mad at each other for long because they can’t stay away from each other for long. They are best friends after all.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: The Physics of Familiarity
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 15,086Chapters: 6/11Kudos: 18

Summary: Lois Lane knows Clark Kent the way you know a city you've lived in for twelve years—by which streets flood in heavy rain, which shortcuts only work on Tuesdays, which silences mean danger and which mean he's just watching a bird. Alfred Pennyworth has been recording Bruce Wayne's vital signs, dietary intake, psychological indicators, and patrol efficiency for thirty years. He has documentation. He has baselines. He has thirty years of knowing exactly what this man looks like. Neither of them recognizes what's sitting across from them at breakfast. At Tuesday lunch. In the study at 6 AM in Thomas Wayne's Edinburgh sweater, holding coffee with both hands, watching the garden like it's somewhere he's allowed to be. They came to the same conclusion by different routes: something is wrong. They're beginning to suspect they might be wrong about that.

entry.title: operation taming (romancing) the red hood
entry.summary:
Author: alleousai

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,350Chapters: 1/?Kudos: 37

Summary: The last thing Peter needed was a multiversal turn-around in the middle of America's Crime Capital, with his New York and secret identity at risk and his mental health down the drain. Lost and alone, he finds an abandoned coffee shop in Crime Alley and decides to make a home (and hideout) out of it until further notice, before a big guy dressed down in leather and quoting Jane Austen stumbles inside and demands recompense. One day is fine. Until the city's eyes are on him and the once-run down coffee shop becomes a haven for superheroes and civilians and metahumans alike, and all Peter wants to do is go back home and save New York. Tangled in a web of adventure, secrets and lies, the Red Hood won't let him go, nor will the Batfamily. Peter detonating won't be too bad of an idea, right? He couldn't be further from the truth. And Jason Todd trusted him enough to put him back together.

  • Graphic Depictions Of Violence
